title: MMR BTCUSDT 1h: BTC mean-reversion from EMA200-extreme with extended maxBarsHeld
content: BTC has different mean-reversion characteristics than ETH: (1) BTC is the benchmark asset — mean-reversions from EMA200-extreme may be more reliable because BTC's larger liquidity means extreme displacements are more likely to be temporary; (2) BTC 1h has different ATR-to-move ratios than ETH. Prior ETH 1h tests showed NO_EDGE with default maxBarsHeld=12 due to 12h being too short. This hypothesis applies the extended-maxBarsHeld fix to BTC 1h. MECHANISM: BTC extreme displacements from EMA200 in the 1h timeframe, when combined with RSI oversold, represent institutional-level capitulation events that statistically revert to EMA50 within 24-96 hours. Extending maxBarsHeld to 48-96 bars allows the engine to capture this reversion rather than force-exiting after 12h. AXES: extremeAtrMult[1.5-3.0] x stopAtrBuffer[0.6-1.8] x maxBarsHeld[24-96]. This hypothesis is independent of the ETH 1h and ETH 4h MMR tests — different instrument with different market microstructure.

structured_data.motivation: BTC mean-reversion from EMA200-extreme; extended maxBarsHeld allows 48-96h hold for reversion completion
